subject: English
title: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom

questions.text: When was the inauguration day of Nelson Mandela's presidency?
questions.explanation: 10 May dawned bright and clear as the day of the inauguration in Pretoria.

questions.text: The inauguration ceremony took place in which city?
questions.explanation: It took place in the lovely sandstone amphitheatre formed by the Union Buildings in Pretoria.

questions.text: What does Mandela call the 'rainbow gathering of different colors and nations'?
questions.explanation: Leaders from more than 140 countries came to witness the installation of South Africa's first democratic government.

questions.text: Mandela was accompanied on the podium by his daughter named:
questions.explanation: Zenani accompanied him on that historic autumn day.

questions.text: Who was sworn in as the first deputy president?
questions.explanation: Thabo Mbeki was sworn in as first deputy president, while Mr de Klerk was second deputy president.

questions.text: What is the policy of 'apartheid'?
options: Political system that separates people according to their race
questions.explanation: Apartheid was a brutal system of racial discrimination practiced in South Africa.

questions.text: Mandela said that the country is rich in minerals and gems, but its greatest wealth is:
questions.explanation: He believed people are finer and truer than the purest diamonds.

questions.text: From whom did Mandela learn the meaning of courage?
questions.explanation: He saw men and women risk and give their lives for an idea during the struggle.

questions.text: According to Mandela, what is courage?
questions.explanation: The brave man is not he who does not feel afraid, but he who conquers that fear.

questions.text: Mandela says that no one is born ______ another person because of the color of his skin.
questions.explanation: He believed people must learn to hate, and if they can learn to hate, they can be taught to love.

questions.text: Mandela mentions that every man has 'twin obligations'. These are towards his family and towards:
options: His people, his community, and his country
questions.explanation: A person has duties to both their private life and their public role in society.

questions.text: What was 'extraordinary human disaster' that Mandela mentions in his speech?
questions.explanation: Apartheid lasted too long and caused deep and lasting wounds to the people.

questions.text: What did the South African jets, helicopters and troop carriers demonstrate over the Union Buildings?
options: Military might and loyalty to democracy
questions.explanation: It was a display of pinpoint precision and military loyalty to the new government.

questions.text: Why were two national anthems sung?
options: To show the unity of blacks and whites
questions.explanation: Singing both 'Nkosi Sikelel’ iAfrika' and 'Die Stem' symbolized the end of racial segregation.

questions.text: What realization did Mandela have of his boyhood freedom?
questions.explanation: As a student, he realized that his freedom was restricted and his 'boyhood freedom' was just a dream.

questions.text: What began Mandela's hunger for freedom?
options: The fact that it had already been taken from him
questions.explanation: His desire for the dignity of his people transformed him into a bold revolutionary.

questions.text: A man who takes away another man's freedom is a:
questions.explanation: Mandela believed the oppressor is as much a prisoner as the oppressed.

questions.text: The 'spectacular array of South African jets' left a smoke trail of the new flag's colors. These colors were:
options: Black, Red, Green, Gold and Blue
questions.explanation: The colors of the new South African flag were displayed in the flypast.

questions.text: Mandela joined which organization to fight for freedom?
questions.explanation: He joined the ANC when he realized not only his freedom, but the freedom of everyone like him was curtailed.

questions.text: What did Mandela feel was 'born of an extraordinary human disaster'?
options: A new society of which all humanity will be proud
questions.explanation: He hoped the new South Africa would be a beacon of hope for the world.

questions.text: The structure created by Apartheid was one of the 'harshest, most in-human societies the world has ever known'. This structure was based on:
questions.explanation: It was a system of white supremacy over the black-skinned people of their own land.

questions.text: Who are the 'unimaginable sacrifices of thousands of my people' that Mandela refers to?
options: The freedom fighters who died before the inauguration
questions.explanation: He felt he was the 'sum of all those African patriots' who had gone before him.

questions.text: According to Mandela, love comes more ______ to the human heart than its opposite.
questions.explanation: He believed that love is a more innate human emotion than hate.

questions.text: A man who is deprived of his twin obligations is forced to live a life of:
questions.explanation: In South Africa, a black man attempting to fulfill his duty to his people was ripped from his family.

questions.text: What were the 'transitory freedoms' Mandela wanted as a young man?
options: Freedom to stay out at night and read what he pleased
questions.explanation: These were temporary, personal freedoms he desired before understanding the larger struggle.

questions.text: What changed a 'frightened young man' into a 'bold one'?
options: The desire for his own wealth
The hunger for the freedom of his people
questions.explanation: The desire for the dignity of his race transformed his entire character.

questions.text: Mandela says 'the oppressed and the oppressor alike are robbed of their ______'.
questions.explanation: He believed both are prisoners—one of bars, the other of prejudice and narrow-mindedness.

questions.text: The decade in which the Apartheid system was overturned was the:
questions.explanation: South Africa became a democracy in 1994.

questions.text: Mandela describes the day of inauguration as 'autumn day'. Why?
options: It was in the month of October
It was in May (Southern Hemisphere autumn)
It was a metaphor for old age
questions.explanation: In the Southern Hemisphere, May falls during the autumn season.

questions.text: How many years did Mandela spend in prison?
questions.explanation: Nelson Mandela spent nearly three decades in prison for his fight against white minority rule.

questions.text: Oliver Tambo, Walter Sisulu, and Chief Luthuli were Mandela's:
options: Patriots and comrades in the struggle
questions.explanation: These were men of extraordinary courage and wisdom who led the struggle with him.

questions.text: South Africa was 'outlaws' not so long ago because:
options: It was in a state of war
questions.explanation: International nations had broken off diplomatic relations due to the country's racist policies.

questions.text: What does Mandela pledge to liberate his people from?
questions.explanation: His speech focused on ending all forms of bondage and discrimination.

questions.text: What transformed a 'family-loving husband' into a 'man without a home'?
questions.explanation: Commitment to his people forced him to live the life of a fugitive and prisoner.

questions.text: Mandela says 'freedom is indivisible'. What does he mean?
options: Freedom is for everyone or for no one
Freedom is only for the rich
The chains on one were the chains on all
questions.explanation: He believed that as long as any of his people were not free, he was not free.

questions.text: Who is the 'prisoner of hatred'?
questions.explanation: Mandela argued that the one who hates is locked behind the bars of prejudice.

questions.text: The Union Buildings amphitheatre was the seat of ______ for decades.
questions.explanation: It was previously the center of the racist Apartheid government.

questions.text: Mandela was South Africa's first ______ president.
questions.explanation: He was elected after the first multi-racial elections in the country's history.

questions.text: What is the name of Mandela's autobiography?
questions.explanation: The chapter is an extract from his famous autobiography of the same name.

questions.text: The 'depths of oppression' create 'heights of ______'.
questions.explanation: Mandela observed that extreme suffering often produced men of extraordinary quality and spirit.
